subject: [PATCH 3/4] mm: page_alloc: move capture_control to the page allocator

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch moves the per-task capture_control to the page allocator, allowing it to carry allocation-side information that compaction's compact_control does not. The capture_control is passed through try_to_compact_pages() and compact_zone_order() instead of a bare struct page **. This change is necessary because defrag_mode promotes sub-block allocations to pageblock-order compaction, which targets the larger order while capture should remain at the original allocation order. The patch makes no functional changes.

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer suggested an alternative approach for handling whole-pageblock pages, but noted that the current patch also works

Reviewer Vlastimil suggested simplifying the code by removing the capc->cc struct and accessing only necessary fields directly in try_to_compact_pages(), which would eliminate the need for compact_zone_order() to set up capc fields.

Reviewer Vlastimil (SUSE) questioned the necessity of explicit initialization of all fields in the capture_control struct, suggesting that either implicit initialization with a barrier is sufficient or every field should be explicitly set to NULL/zero.
reviews.raw_body:
Well I guess you could also try to capture the whole-pageblock page and then
deal with it as with whole pageblock stealing? But this works too and
perhaps it's simpler.

---

I wonder if it makes sense to continue having capc->cc and this whole dance
in two functions. AFAICS (after patch 4/4) we access only capc->cc->zone and
capc->cc->migratetype. migratetype is stable in the whole
try_to_compact_pages(), could be part of capc. Order can be added by this
patch (with no semantic change to it) and not the next one. Zone varies, but
could be also in capc and set by try_to_compact_pages() before every call to
compact_zone_order(). Then compact_zone_order() doesn't have to set up any
capc fields anymore?

---

So either an implicit { } NULL / zero initialization + barrier() is enough
(I hope so) and we don't need to set NULL / zero in every field explicitly.
Or not and then we should set every field and not just page.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2

Author addressed a concern about the capture_control struct being passed through try_to_compact_pages() and compact_zone_order(), explaining that they added 'struct zone' and 'int migratetype' fields to it, and provided an updated diff showing these changes.

Author acknowledged that initializing capture_control partially would zero out its other fields, but didn't see it as a correctness issue; no fix planned.

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch moves the per-task capture_control to the page allocator, allowing it to carry allocation-side information that compaction's compact_control does not. The capture_control is passed through try_to_compact_pages() and compact_zone_order() instead of a bare struct page **. This change is necessary because defrag_mode promotes sub-block allocations to pageblock-order compaction, which targets the larger order, while capture should remain at the original allocation order. No functional changes are made by this patch.

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch changes capc->zone in an 'armed' state, but believes it is safe because an interrupt can only see a compatible zone

Reviewer noted that the capture_control struct is initialized implicitly, and suggested using an empty initializer instead of explicitly setting its fields to NULL or 0; provided a patch with this change
sentiment_signals: no clear signal of approval or disapproval
